[2018-03-15 23:46] liadahlia (Lia): is it worth buying a nivo every turn just to deny absorb on matrix?
[2018-03-15 23:47] wilson0862 (Wilson): Why not just buy 10 nivos? 🤔
[2018-03-15 23:47] wilson0862 (Wilson): And deny absorb on engis? 🤔
[2018-03-15 23:47] liadahlia (Lia): ~3 Gold per turn to deny 2 absorb which is worth ~4.5 gold depending on the set
[2018-03-15 23:47] liadahlia (Lia): i mean what if you're just not setup to buy 10 nivos on a turn /shrug
[2018-03-15 23:47] Deleted User (Deleted User): think nivo to deny matrix is proper
[2018-03-15 23:48] liadahlia (Lia): also opponent has to overdefend by 5
[2018-03-15 23:48] wilson0862 (Wilson): They get the defence back though (obviously).
[2018-03-15 23:48] mqp: nivo per turn to deny absorb on matrix owns
[2018-03-15 23:48] wilson0862 (Wilson): It can be quite good but it always depends.
[2018-03-15 23:48] liadahlia (Lia): what if opponent just buys another matrix
[2018-03-15 23:48] liadahlia (Lia): then you gotta buy 2 nivos a turn
[2018-03-15 23:48] wilson0862 (Wilson): ya I am very likely to buy that first one.
[2018-03-15 23:48] mqp: if they can do that easily then the nivo was not as good
[2018-03-15 23:49] mqp: but in a matrix game it will be proper to buy all the nivos eventually to freeze these matrix, the only question is when
[2018-03-15 23:50] mqp: if you have an opportunity to buy just one at a time when they can't easily un-deny the absorb by buying second matrix (e.g. the turn right before they buy the first matrix, or if they have no more matrix in supply), do it for sure though

[2018-03-16 06:32] zugz: hey guys can i get some thoughts on how i played this
[2018-03-16 06:33] zugz: i think my opponent just owned himself by droning way too hard but im curious if my line was good
[2018-03-16 06:34] mtanzer: opening looks good.  you don't really want a second animus though, it probably just makes nitrocybes if you can even spend it
[2018-03-16 06:36] zugz: even with only 12 gold i was having trouble spending it all
[2018-03-16 06:36] zugz: since hellhounds are so gold-cheap but tech-heavy
[2018-03-16 06:38] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): BBBA and 17 ish drones sounds good to me
[2018-03-16 06:38] mtanzer: you probably just want engis/drones if that's the case.  you can only buy so many hounds until attack gets high enough that you're just buying soak, and when that happens you usually find that you don't want to have a second animus
[2018-03-16 06:38] mtanzer: you can't get apollo in that set.  17 drones too much
[2018-03-16 06:38] zugz: there was corpus too so i had lots of sinks for extra red
[2018-03-16 06:38] zugz: at least this was my thinking
[2018-03-16 06:38] zugz: not saying you're wrong that was my reasoning at the time
[2018-03-16 06:39] zugz: idk what id do with extra drones
[2018-03-16 06:39] mtanzer: I think what happened is you were kinda ahead and didn't have to buy as much soak as usual.  normally buying wall and corpus/cybe/engi would spend everything after getting a few hound turns
[2018-03-16 06:40] randomflyingtaco (RiFT (#RIPBrowserers)): I guess I'd try to get Apollo, give up and just buy 10 hh instead
[2018-03-16 06:40] mtanzer: sometimes people get a second forge in hound sets.  it's more useful for soak later, but I think recently people have moved away from it in favor of 1 forge 1 animus in hound sets
[2018-03-16 06:41] zugz: in that case should i have gone even lower on drones
[2018-03-16 06:41] mtanzer: the turn you bought the second animus you could just buy another tarsier and an engi or drone
[2018-03-16 06:41] mtanzer: I guess you didn't have energy so an engi then
[2018-03-16 06:42] mtanzer: hound+tarsier+engi per turn costs 11.  and 2 engis would be 13.  you were on 12 income so that looks fine until you need to buy more soak
[2018-03-16 06:42] zugz: i think you're right that my opponent just straight didn't pressure me
[2018-03-16 06:43] zugz: or i would have had to spend my gold
[2018-03-16 06:44] mtanzer: np.  yeah like without the pressure you don't get punished as hard for the extra animus, but even without the pressure you still have nice turns on 1 animus like I just said
[2018-03-16 06:54] Deleted User (Deleted User): think 2 forge in most hound sets is proper

[2018-03-16 07:52] zugz: while im asking a bunch of questions
[2018-03-16 07:52] zugz: what is the proper use of ebb turbine
[2018-03-16 07:52] zugz: i cant figure that unit out
[2018-03-16 07:52] namington: it's 2 drones, basically
[2018-03-16 07:52] namington: for -1 energy but +1 b
[2018-03-16 07:53] namington: but in the late game, drones are worth less than attackers
[2018-03-16 07:53] namington: (that's why people recommend you stop buying drones and transition to attack at some point)
[2018-03-16 07:53] namington: ebb "refunds" your drones, allowing you to convert them into attackers in the late game
[2018-03-16 07:53] namington: exactly when to start doing this is tough, though
[2018-03-16 07:55] zugz: so should i even buy it before im ready to start cashing in drones?
[2018-03-16 07:56] crash_overlord: It's a good investment early instead of splitter if splitter doesn't do anything
[2018-03-16 07:57] mqp: ebb is a great unit to have 1 of after the "halfway point" in the game where you don't want more drones and want attack and defense
[2018-03-16 07:57] crash_overlord: And you start clicking it quite early tbh
[2018-03-16 07:57] mqp: it's also great in situations where there is one high-tech unit you want but you won't have enough economy to spend all the tech after that
[2018-03-16 07:57] mqp: like vai, lucina, colossus
[2018-03-16 07:57] crash_overlord: Gets you big units faster too (odin, makes antima bigger, amporilla...)
[2018-03-16 07:58] mqp: like if you can click the ebb to spend a blue or red profitably that you otherwise would have burned, that was pretty sweet

[2018-03-18 01:57] iminabearsuit: If you're attacking for 3 into a wall and 2 engineers. You have a single pixie, and are unable to build anything more. Saving the pixie allows for a better exploit on turn 2.
[2018-03-18 01:58] mqp: consider various cases
[2018-03-18 01:59] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): It does if opponent doesn't replace engis and you attack for an amount that forces 1 health granularity to keep dying
[2018-03-18 01:59] f300xen (f0x): engineers are not prompt, so you should get rid of them with pixies if possible
[2018-03-18 02:00] mqp: ok spiritfryer in your example
[2018-03-18 02:00] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But if you can just get more pixies then might as well kill the engis now and get more pixies for exploit
[2018-03-18 02:00] mqp: A) you click pixie, he loses engineers onto wall turn 1, he loses wall turn 2, getting exploited for 2, he loses wall turn 3, getting exploited for 2
[2018-03-18 02:01] .bky_1556 (bky): To put it a different way, if you have pixies and the opponent has an equal number of engineers, the opponent can ensure those pixies kill those specific engineers no matter what, by defender's choice.
[2018-03-18 02:01] mqp: B) you click pixie, he loses wall onto wall turn 1, getting exploited for 1, he loses engineer onto wall turn 2, he loses engineer onto wall turn 3, he loses wall turn 4 (this one is better for him)
[2018-03-18 02:01] .bky_1556 (bky): They might also have better options.
[2018-03-18 02:02] .bky_1556 (bky): So if you CAN trade one pixie for one engineer, you do it,
[2018-03-18 02:02] mqp: C) you don't click pixie, he loses engineer onto wall turn 1, now you click pixie, he loses wall onto wall turn 2, he loses engineer onto wall turn 3, he loses wall turn 4 (same as B)
[2018-03-18 02:02] .bky_1556 (bky): because you can only get a worse deal for that pixie later.
[2018-03-18 02:02] .bky_1556 (bky): (Exception: Frontline, where defender's choice doesn't happen)
[2018-03-18 02:02] mqp: D) you don't click pixie, he loses engineer onto wall turn 1, you still don't click pixie, he loses engineer onto wall turn 2, he loses wall turn 3, he loses wall turn 4, but you still have the pixie...hmmmmmmmm
[2018-03-18 02:03] mqp: ok D was actually better somehow
[2018-03-18 02:03] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well this is a very minimalist case
[2018-03-18 02:03] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): In most cases there are multiple Pixies and multiple Engis
[2018-03-18 02:03] mqp: oh i was assuming 2 walls by the way sorry
[2018-03-18 02:04] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And if you click x pixies, then all of a sudden they don't all kill Engis anymore etc.
[2018-03-18 02:04] iminabearsuit: Yes, but if you don't have pixies anymore, you have a predictable attack number.
[2018-03-18 02:04] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Though I guess then opponent is overdefended
[2018-03-18 02:04] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): (If not all pixies kill engis when clicked)
[2018-03-18 02:04] iminabearsuit: It's better to force the opponent to defend against as many different of numbers possible.
[2018-03-18 02:05] mqp: ok this was an interesting case
[2018-03-18 02:05] mqp: holding the pixie was better because it didn't kill an engineer
[2018-03-18 02:05] mqp: if you click the pixie he loses wall onto wall
[2018-03-18 02:06] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Actually the tough part is when you have pixies, and your non-pixie damage total is such that opponent doesn't need 1 health granu to defend it
[2018-03-18 02:06] mqp: i still assert that if pixie kills an engineer it was correct to click
[2018-03-18 02:06] iminabearsuit: Nah, pixie is worth more than one damage. Unless the energy is going to be used.
[2018-03-18 02:06] iminabearsuit: And sure, you can assume the opponent will exploit himself against your non pixie attack numbers...
[2018-03-18 02:06] iminabearsuit: But that won't really happen.
[2018-03-18 02:07] f300xen (f0x): isn't it pretty pointless to click pixie if they're already absorbing for their max amount and clicking it doesn't change that
[2018-03-18 02:07] mqp: bearsuit if you can show me an example where pixie kills an engineer but was incorrect to click (for granularity reasons -- notwithstanding freeze, arka, etc.) i'll paypal you twenty bucks
[2018-03-18 02:07] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well only if Energy (or Engi existence for Tesla) is useless @f0x
[2018-03-18 02:07] .bky_1556 (bky): If you have a ton of pixies and enough attackers to overcome their absorb barrier, it tends to be almost all or nothing.
[2018-03-18 02:08] mqp: it doesn't make sense to me, because my perspective is that he's got this engineer and you've got the pixie and he will keep the engineer forever until you click the pixie
[2018-03-18 02:08] .bky_1556 (bky): Medium amounts of pixie poppage give them more choice about how to soak.
[2018-03-18 02:08] f300xen (f0x): well obviously we're assuming that energy is useless
[2018-03-18 02:08] mqp: he will not "run out of granularity" sooner because you didn't click the pixie to kill the granularity
[2018-03-18 02:08] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But keeping the Pixies up makes your opponent have to consider an extra thing on defense
[2018-03-18 02:09] f300xen (f0x): well if he has only 1 engi and clicking pixie kills that engi you should probably click it so he can't have granularity
[2018-03-18 02:09] .bky_1556 (bky): So you usually want to either click just enough pixies to burn granularity, or click enough pixies that the opponent *must* sacrifice a specific unit, but rarely do you want to burn a medium number of pixies.
[2018-03-18 02:09] Deleted User (Deleted User): pretty sure u always sac a pixie to kill an E frontline/attack payment/chill aside
[2018-03-18 02:09] wilson0862 (Wilson): This is a funny conversation because it doesn't matter 94.2% of the time.
[2018-03-18 02:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Nah Cooled, only follow that rule if you have more Pixies incoming imo
[2018-03-18 02:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Otherwise your attack total has to be considered
[2018-03-18 02:10] f300xen (f0x): never sac pixies unless you're dealing the damage to something important, can that just be agreed on
[2018-03-18 02:10] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Cos if opponent doesn't need 1 health granu to defend it, then it may be better to set up an attack total that does require 1 hp granu to be defended
[2018-03-18 02:10] Deleted User (Deleted User): ah that makes sense spirit

[2018-03-18 12:26] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): If you have a Xaetron which makes all your opponent's freeze real, but if they freeze non-Xaetron then Xae is not breached, but you also have breach-vulnerable units like Tarsiers, then how much would you overdefend for to save Tarsiers in general?

I'm looking for an approximate count of how many Frostbites sacrificed per Tarsier killed would I be ok with?
[2018-03-18 12:29] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Let's assume Xae has 12 health. 4 Bites would then deal 4 damage. They would like to deal 8. I guess killing a Tarsier is worth more than 1 damage, if the game will go on for a while still. So I would assume in such a case I wouldn't want to gambit more than 1 Tarsier for the 4 bites
[2018-03-18 12:30] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And I suppose it also depends on how awkward my defensive options are
[2018-03-18 12:31] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): And how big of an absorber I'm losing if Xaetron is frozen
[2018-03-18 12:32] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So since there are many factors it's probably easier to discuss how much damage am I ok with each Bite dealing, and how much damage killing a Tarsier is worth for considering the damage dealt per Bite
[2018-03-18 12:32] mtanzer: in general I think 2 damage for a bite is fair value
[2018-03-18 12:32] mtanzer: I think of killing a tarsier as being worth 3 damage or so since I think 4 damage to kill an attacker is about fair
[2018-03-18 12:33] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): So if my Xae is at 12, and my absorber that I gambited for exact breach is wall, then 4 Bites would deal 6 damage, so gambit for exact in such a case would be good vs 4 bites
[2018-03-18 12:33] mtanzer: it would only deal 4 damage?
[2018-03-18 12:34] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Xae doesn't heal 4, and wall doesn't absorb 2
[2018-03-18 12:34] mtanzer: if xae is at 12 then wall wasn't absorbing either way
[2018-03-18 12:35] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): How big a difference would it be if instead of Wall it was an Infusion Grid or an Energy Matrix?
[2018-03-18 12:36] mtanzer: not sure exactly what the question is
[2018-03-18 12:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Well in those cases the damage dealt would still be 4
[2018-03-18 12:37] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But I'm losing more valuable absorbers
[2018-03-18 12:37] mtanzer: if we're freezing a xae at 12 that wanted to block then we're denying 4 heal
[2018-03-18 12:37] mtanzer: regardless of the absorber?
[2018-03-18 12:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): I think it's difficult to discuss this in an abstract way
[2018-03-18 12:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Better if I have an actual example
[2018-03-18 12:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): But regardless, ty for pointing out the "if 12 hp Xae, then absorber wouldn't absorb anyway"
[2018-03-18 12:38] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Learnt something already
[2018-03-18 12:40] mtanzer: cool glad to help.  yeah xae can be hard to think about abstractly
[2018-03-18 12:40] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Also seems to me that Xae is one of the strongest counters to Chill
[2018-03-18 12:41] spiritfryer (Spyrfyr): Especially if the absorber is bigger than Wall
[2018-03-18 12:41] mtanzer: on turns when you were gonna overdefend xae to get absorb this deals with chill nicely yeah
[2018-03-18 12:41] mtanzer: on turns where you were gonna block on xae though chill still forces out more soak
